svg.text: L3 LangGraph agent: analyses a test suite and produces an optimised plan; recommends only, pauses for human approval 3×. Provider: Google Gemini (offline-safe).
BROWSER · REACT SPA (Vite dev server, http://localhost:5173)
passes props down · gets callbacks up
types.ts — TypeScript shapes for every request/payload/output (compile-time contract; no runtime code). index.css — all styling. main.tsx — mounts <App/>.
POST /runs — start a run; blocks until 1st checkpoint or completion → returns {run_id, status, checkpoint, payload | outputs}
POST /runs/{id}/resume — submit a HITL decision (raw shape; backend wraps {"__hitl__": …}) → next checkpoint or outputs
BACKEND · FASTAPI BRIDGE (uvicorn, http://127.0.0.1:8000) — thin shell, no agent logic
MemorySaver checkpointer keyed by thread_id (= run_id) → pause at interrupt(), resume across requests
recommend-only: never commits / deletes; warms the Gemini SDK at startup
LANGGRAPH AGENT · src/graph.py · state = TestOptimiserState (append-only audit_log / tool_errors)
parse + normalise suite, extract entities; isolate unparseable
cluster near-duplicates; flag flaky / slow from CI history
6 health dimensions (LLM, strict JSON; else deterministic)
re-tier smoke / regression / full by risk + goal
interrupt(): confirm the tiering + projected coverage
draft a test per gap (LLM; else stub); gen_retry_count++
sandbox syntax/import check → retry / drop / approve
interrupt(): pick generated tests to keep
render 4 deliverables; tag addressed gaps; write memory
CAPABILITY LAYERS (nodes call them via call_tool)
vector_store (Chroma) · sandbox (subprocess syntax/import — never runs prod)
degrade rules: coverage→estimate · CI→"needs data" · repo→fatal halt
used by: intake · coverage · redundancy · retrieval · validation
src/nlp/ — deterministic backbone (no token cost; offline fallback)
gemini-2.5-flash (REASONING_MODEL / FAST_MODEL) · 429 / no key → deterministic fallback
DATA INPUTS · sample_data/ (generated, single source of truth)
sample_suite/*.py — the test suite (23 tests, 5 files) ← suite_path (per-run)
sample_criteria.json — acceptance criteria AC-* (FIXED path → test_management)
expected_findings.json — GOLDEN answer key (test-only; not read at runtime)
generate_sample_data.py — declares planted facts, writes all of the above
consumed by the Tools layer; only suite_path is request-driven
OUTPUTS · written to outputs/*.json (CLI) · returned in the run response (API → Result tabs)
＋ append-only audit_log (every node/event) · tool_errors (degraded dependencies → DegradedBanner) · approved decisions + new flaky tests persisted to memory
The API response carries these back to App.tsx, which renders the 4 Result tabs; the CLI writes them as four JSON files in outputs/.
• Backend is synchronous: a POST blocks until the next interrupt() or END. Resume decisions are raw (list / tiers / list); api.py wraps them as {"__hitl__": …} so an empty approval still resumes.

• Two bounded loops: coverage_floor_gate ⇄ revise (never lets a change set drop below the 0.80 coverage target); validation ⇄ gap_generation (≤ MAX_GEN_RETRIES=3) then drop_failing — the run always terminates.
• Three HITL interrupts gate every destructive action; risk-area tests are pinned (never removable). Nothing is deleted/committed without a human decision written into state.
• Offline-safe: with no Gemini key (or on 429), scoring + gap_generation fall back to deterministic methods and the run still completes — surfaced via tool_errors / the degraded banner.
• Single source of truth: sample_data is generated; the e2e test (tests/test_graph_e2e.py) asserts the agent reproduces expected_findings.json. AGENT_SPEC.md is the design authority.
